verdict: PARTIAL — 5 issues still open, 2 HIGH, 1 CRITICAL-CLASS. Score below 8.5 threshold.

criteria_scores.name: Rendu 3D — flat-shading cohérence, z-fighting, matériaux, lighting
criteria_scores.finding: flatShading:true used consistently on pedestal, crystal, books, ground, ocean — good cohérence. DEFECT: crystal sphere at MerlinLairScene.ts:166 uses SphereGeometry(0.7, 16, 16) — 16 segments produce a visually round sphere. With flatShading:true the facets become visible only below ~6 segments; at 16 the surface looks smooth, breaking low-poly style parity with all other primitives. Pedestal CylinderGeometry(0.4, 0.55, 0.9, 8) correctly uses 8 segments — crystal should match.
criteria_scores.recommendation: Reduce SphereGeometry segments from (16,16) to (6,5) to match flat-shaded facet aesthetic.

criteria_scores.name: Assets — GLB coverage, proportions, Celtic style
criteria_scores.finding: CoastBiome.ts lines 1-80 shows procedural geometry only (PlaneGeometry for ground, ocean) with no GLB asset loaded in this window. loadGLB is imported but not yet called within the first 80 lines — cones/trees relying on procedural geometry maintain Celtic mood but lack the GLB asset density seen in MerlinLairScene. Celtic mood (overcast shader, grey-green palette) is authentic.
criteria_scores.recommendation: Confirm GLB assets are loaded after line 80; if coast remains fully procedural, add at least one GLB menhir/dolmen to differentiate from ForestBiome.

criteria_scores.name: Web Perf — bundle gzip, FPS, memory leaks
criteria_scores.finding: Minigame lazy imports confirmed at main.ts:16 comment — dynamic import defers ~21KB chunk. LLM fallback card generation (main.ts:390-406) prevents hangs. CoastBiome PlaneGeometry uses reasonable vertex counts (48x48, 24x18). No dispose() calls audited in the 80-line window for CoastBiome, but geometry is procedural and scene-scoped. No Memory leak detected in audited files.
criteria_scores.recommendation: Add dispose() to CoastBiome geometries/materials if not present after line 80.

criteria_scores.name: UX/Accessibility — ARIA, keyboard nav, focus, mobile touch
criteria_scores.finding: C59 keyboard fix confirmed landed: role=button + tabindex=0 at CardOverlay.ts:163-164, keydown handler Enter/Space at lines 196-200, requestAnimationFrame focus-first-option at lines 210-212 — all present and correct. LLM loading hint has role=status + aria-live=polite at main.ts:515-516 — good. DEFECT: CardOverlay.ts lines 9-12 still use 4 non-null assertion closures (`!`) — overlay(), textEl(), optionsEl() all call getElementById(...)!. If the DOM element is absent (race condition or SSR hydration), these throw at runtime with no guard. The cardContainer() closure at line 12 correctly uses querySelector<HTMLElement> without `!` but also silently returns null — callers use optional chaining (?.), so that one is safe.
criteria_scores.recommendation: Replace the three `!` closures with null-safe variants that return null and add early-return guards at the call sites in showCard() and hideCard().

criteria_scores.name: Game Feel — card interaction, loading feedback, minigame entry
criteria_scores.finding: Card flip CSS animation triggered each new card (CardOverlay.ts:117-127). Gold highlight on selection (card-option-selected, 200ms, line 187). LLM loading hint shown/hidden cleanly around Groq call (main.ts:386-392). Fallback card generation ensures no freeze on LLM timeout. MINOR: LLM loading hint uses inline cssText strings (main.ts:517-531) — not critical but brittle vs a CSS class.
criteria_scores.recommendation: Move LLM hint styles to a CSS class for maintainability.

criteria_scores.name: Game Design — lore cohérence, faction transparency, progression
criteria_scores.finding: BUG-C59-03 still open. buildFactionDot() at CardOverlay.ts:100-111 iterates effects and returns on the FIRST ADD_REPUTATION match (line 108: `return dot`). A dual-faction card (e.g., +druides:12, -ankou:8) renders only the green druides dot — the negative ankou impact is invisible to the player. This is a transparency failure: players cannot see negative faction consequences before committing to a choice. The effect-tooltip (buildEffectTooltip) correctly shows ALL effects (lines 76-91), so tooltip is fine — the problem is isolated to the dot indicator.
criteria_scores.recommendation: Remove the early return from buildFactionDot; collect all ADD_REPUTATION effects and render one dot per faction, or rename the function to buildFactionDots (plural) returning HTMLElement[].

issues.issue: buildFactionDot() returns on first ADD_REPUTATION match — dual-faction cards hide negative impacts
issues.recommendation: Collect all ADD_REPUTATION effects; render one dot per faction. Return type: HTMLElement[] instead of HTMLElement | null.

issues.issue: SphereGeometry(0.7, 16, 16) — 16-segment sphere looks round, not low-poly, breaks flat-shading cohérence
issues.recommendation: Change to SphereGeometry(0.7, 6, 5) to produce visible facets consistent with the pedestal and bookshelf geometry.

issues.issue: 4 non-null assertion closures at CardOverlay.ts:9-11 — overlay(), textEl(), optionsEl() throw uncaught TypeError if DOM element absent
issues.recommendation: Replace `getElementById('x')!` with null-safe wrappers; add early return guards in showCard() and hideCard().

issues.issue: LLM loading hint uses 12-part inline cssText string — brittle, not covered by theme changes
issues.recommendation: Extract to a CSS class llm-loading-hint in the stylesheet.

open_from_previous_cycles.note: dual-faction dot — not addressed in C60 code
crystal sphere segment count unchanged at 16,16

resolved_since_c59.resolution: Keyboard fix confirmed: role=button, tabindex=0, keydown Enter/Space, requestAnimationFrame focus — all present at CardOverlay.ts:163-212
showLLMLoadingHint / hideLLMLoadingHint implemented at main.ts:507-538 with role=status aria-live=polite

balance_recommendations.impact: visual style cohérence with flat-shading system
runtime crash prevention on DOM race
